refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: deriving, recording, and managing performance requirements. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these were separated into distinct sub-FRs. The refined FR focuses solely on the derivation aspect, which is a fundamental functional requirement in the problem domain without implying any implementation.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can analyze worksite mission context inputs and produce a documented set of performance requirements such as cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability, without specifying how this is done.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall record derived performance requirements.
The NGHE solution shall manage the history of performance requirements and calibration updates.

candidate_dps: Define a logical model to translate worksite mission context into performance requirements
Establish a traceable mapping framework linking worksite inputs to performance targets
Implement a version-controlled repository for performance baselines and calibration updates
Develop a validation process to ensure performance targets comply with safety plans and operational policies
selected_dp.selected_dp: Define a logical model to translate worksite mission context into performance requirements

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical model that isolates the translation of mission context to performance requirements, supporting independence from implementation details and other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes worksite mission context inputs are well-defined and accessible
Risk that the logical model may become overly complex if too many variables are considered
Assumes safety plans and operational policies are stable and can be integrated into the model
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a DP for maintaining traceability and history of performance targets to fully meet acceptance criteria and support calibration updates.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Logical model to translate worksite mission context into performance requirements

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives data about current worksite conditions and vehicle status

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission context and task assignments

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts if performance requirements impact safety

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with other system modules and external systems

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the logical model is central to the overall solution; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Vehicle module is more about physical control and execution; logical model would diffuse significantly outside.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control; logical model would be poorly contained here.
Safety Services may use performance requirements but does not define the logical model; moderate diffusion expected.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the logical model translating mission context into performance requirements, as it centralizes the system-level logic and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if safety-related performance requirements are tightly coupled.
Interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation may require careful interface management to avoid diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by defining a model that translates the worksite mission context into performance requirements, directly fulfilling the need to derive performance requirements from mission context.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if safety-related performance requirements are tightly coupled.
Interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause diffusion if not carefully managed.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating the logical model is well centralized here. Other modules show poor containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module interfaces provide clear inputs (Telemetry, Tasking) and outputs (SafetyAlert), enabling traceability of performance targets to worksite inputs and history maintenance.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear since the logical model is contained within the NGHE Solution module, which owns the responsibility for translating mission context into performance requirements.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Safety Services to manage potential overlap and ensure clear responsibility boundaries.
Establish interface management protocols with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to prevent diffusion of the logical model across these modules.
Document traceability mechanisms within the NGHE Solution module to satisfy acceptance criteria for performance target documentation and history maintenance.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed, indicating the current level is well defined and logically sound. The NGHE Solution module shows high containment and low diffusion, making it a suitable IM boundary. However, given the high safety criticality and identified diffusion risks with Safety Services, Vehicle, and Teleoperation modules, further decomposition of the FR is warranted to explicitly address these interfaces and responsibilities. This will help manage complexity, clarify allocation, and reduce diffusion risks at a finer granularity.
next_level_sub_frs: Derive sub-FRs to explicitly define interface contracts with Safety Services
Derive sub-FRs to manage interactions with Vehicle module
Derive sub-FRs to manage interactions with Teleoperation module
Derive sub-FRs to document traceability and history maintenance of performance targets

revise.why: To explicitly define and manage interface contracts and protocols between NGHE Solution and related modules to reduce diffusion and clarify ownership.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module is the primary owner of the logical model translating mission context to performance requirements.
Interfaces with Safety Services, Vehicle, and Teleoperation modules are critical and currently pose diffusion risks.
open_risks: Potential overlap and responsibility ambiguity with Safety Services module.
Interface diffusion with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules if not properly managed.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface contracts and protocols between NGHE Solution and related modules.
Traceability documentation within NGHE Solution module for performance targets.
